features.summary: Caterpillar reported Q4 2024 sales of $16.2B (down 5% YoY) and full-year sales of $64.8B (down 3% YoY). The company achieved record adjusted EPS of $21.90 for 2024, up 3% from 2023, with adjusted operating margin of 20.7% exceeding the top of target range. ME&T free cash flow was $9.4B, near the top of the $5-10B target range. Services revenue grew 4% to a record $24B. The company returned $10.3B to shareholders through $7.7B in share repurchases and $2.6B in dividends.
features.management_outlook: For 2025, Caterpillar expects slightly lower sales and revenues compared to 2024, with continued strength in energy & transportation offsetting declines in construction industries and resource industries. The company anticipates adjusted operating profit margins in the top half of the target range and ME&T free cash flow also in the top half of the $5-10B range. Services revenue is expected to grow across all three primary segments. Management expects dealer machine inventory to end 2025 at similar levels to year-end 2024.
features.risks: Key risks include continued weak economic conditions in Europe and Asia Pacific, ongoing capital discipline by resource industry customers despite elevated commodity prices, and unfavorable price realization due to post-sales merchandising programs impacting margins through the first half of 2025. The company faces manufacturing cost headwinds from higher depreciation due to capacity investments and potential absorption issues from lower production volumes. First quarter 2025 margins are expected to be pressured by lower than usual volume and unfavorable pricing.
features.key_drivers: Strong demand for power generation equipment driven by data center growth related to cloud computing and AI is a major growth driver, with the company investing to expand large engine capacity by 125% over 2023 levels. Services growth remains resilient with over 1.5 million connected assets and digital tools driving customer value agreements. The energy transition is expected to support increased commodity demand over time, expanding total addressable market. Autonomous solutions continue to see strong customer acceptance in resource industries.
